title: Iceland - Feel The Elements
creator: Dronographer
award_or_showcase_provenance: DJI/SkyPixel 7th Anniversary Aerial Photo & Video Contest -- 1st Place Video winner (results announced 2022-03-23); one of six named '1st Place Videos' in that year's contest, whose video categories were Nature, City, Travel, FPV, Sport, and Showreel. Secondary sources describe this specific entry as the Nature-category winner and its subject matter (Iceland's natural landscapes) is unambiguously nature/landscape themed, but this session could not trace the exact per-category label to a single directly-quoted primary source -- see notes.
notes: MANUALLY CURATED WORKED EXAMPLE -- one exemplar only, per spec AC3.4/Scope-out (no automated scraping/bulk downloading was performed to produce this record). Verified this session via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=LQtRTF7Iwsk&format=json), which confirmed the exact title ('Iceland - Feel The Elements') and channel/author (Dronographer, https://www.youtube.com/@DronographerEU) for this URL. A direct curl fetch of uavcoach.com's SkyPixel 7th Annual contest writeup (https://uavcoach.com/skypixel-7th-annual/), cross-checked against the official DJI press release (https://www.dji.com/media-center/announcements/skypixel2021-winners-en, published 2022-03-23, ~30,000 submissions from 124 countries/regions), independently confirmed this exact title/creator pairing ('"Iceland-Feel the Elements" Created by SkyPixel contributor Dronographer') as one of six '1st Place Videos' in the SkyPixel 7th Anniversary Aerial Photo & Video Contest. Multiple independent sources confirm the 2021/2022 contest's six video categories were Nature, City, Travel, FPV, Sport, and Showreel, and two independent web-search syntheses characterized this specific entry as the Nature-category winner; the video's subject matter (Iceland's raw natural landscapes -- glaciers, waterfalls, volcanic terrain, geysers, northern lights) is unambiguously nature/landscape themed and does not fit any of the other five category names. However, no single directly-quoted primary source enumerating a per-category (as opposed to per-placement) winner list was found in this session -- the official DJI press release text describes prizes only in aggregate ('the first, second, and third places in all 10 photo and video categories') without naming which video won which specific category, and the underlying SkyPixel contest-results page (skypixel.com/contests/2021) is a JavaScript single-page app that returned no crawlable winner data via curl. Treat the specific 'Nature' sub-category attribution as reasonably well-corroborated (by category-elimination against the other five 2021/2022 category names, all of which are contradicted by this video's title/subject matter, plus two independent secondary-source characterizations) but not confirmed to the same evidentiary standard as the title/creator/contest/placement facts, which are directly sourced. `license_category` is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved': this session fetched the raw YouTube watch-page HTML for this URL and found no 'Creative Commons' or explicit CC-license marking anywhere in the page source, so per this project's stated default (assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise), `local_media_path` stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo. `duration` is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session -- rather than fabricate a plausible-looking number, this field is left unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). `scores` (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are `scores_provenance: manually_estimated` placeholder judgments based on the video's known genre and reputation (professional cinematic aerial landscape footage, contest-winning), NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the same no-bulk-download constraint. `composite_score` IS a real computation: it was produced this session by calling this repo's own `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score` with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.78, exposure=0.75, motion_smoothness=0.82, composition=None) and `drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights()` (weights_version 'default-v1', equal 1/3 weighting on sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness, composition weight fixed at 0.0) via `.venv/bin/python`, yielding composite_score=0.7833333333333333 (rounded to 0.7833 here) -- so the composite arithmetic itself is genuine and reproducible, even though its inputs are estimates rather than measurements.
